title: phacelia malvifolia subsp. loasifolia
intro: Phacelia malvifolia subsp. loasifolia, also known as Loasa phacelia, is a plant species belonging to the hydrophyllaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory issues. The health benefits of Loasa phacelia include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herb for overall wellness. Herbal preparations of Phacelia malvifolia subsp. loasifolia can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for a range of applications in herbal medicine.
